description_long: This is a two-story brick house with a prominent red gable roof and a covered front porch. The exterior walls are finished in reddish-brown brick, and the roof features multiple intersecting gables, giving the structure a classic suburban appearance. Large rectangular windows are symmetrically arranged on both floors, providing ample natural light to the interior. The front entryway is sheltered by a roof extension supported by columns, and the porch is paved with stone tiles. The house sits on a rectangular base, with a clear distinction between the main living area and the attached porch. The overall design is reminiscent of traditional American suburban homes, blending functional and aesthetic elements.
description: A two-story brick house with a red gable roof, large windows, and a covered front porch supported by columns.
description_view_0: Front-left perspective showing the main entryway, porch, and two stories of windows under a red gable roof.
description_view_1: Direct side view highlighting the full expanse of the red roof and the side wall with minimal window detail.
description_view_2: Front-right perspective emphasizing the brick facade, symmetrical window arrangement, and the covered porch entry.
description_view_3: Rear view showing a single large window on the back wall and the full width of the gable roof.
attribute_entryway_design: Covered front porch with columns, tiled steps, and a single main door under a roof overhang
